description_long: This is a large, freestanding retail shelving unit designed for product display, commonly found in urban convenience stores or supermarkets. The structure consists of multiple horizontal shelves, each densely stocked with bottles of various sizes and colors, likely representing beverages such as wine, champagne, or beer. The topmost shelf is filled with neatly arranged cans, forming a uniform layer above the bottles. The shelving unit is constructed from a solid, likely metal or wood frame, painted white, providing a clean and neutral backdrop for the products. The sides and back of the unit are solid panels, while the front is open for easy access to the displayed items. The overall design is utilitarian, maximizing storage and visibility of the products for customers.
description: A large, white retail shelving unit filled with bottles and cans, designed for product display in urban stores. The open front provides easy access to beverages arranged on multiple shelves.
description_view_0: Front view showing all shelves fully stocked with bottles and a top layer of cans, providing a clear view of the product arrangement.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the shelves and the profile of the bottles and cans, with less detail on product labels.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a solid white panel with no visible products or openings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, displaying the side profile of the bottles and cans.
